concepts_list.name: domain_Omega
concepts_list.description: The main computational domain in the physical space, representing the region where the problem is defined.
concepts_list.justification: The domain is explicitly defined as Ω ⊂ R^d, and is referred to multiple times as the domain of interest, with subdomains and boundaries derived from it.

concepts_list.name: primal_variable_u
concepts_list.description: The primary unknown field variable, such as temperature or displacement, depending on the problem context.
concepts_list.justification: The symbol u is used throughout the paper to denote the primal variable, whether in thermal or elastostatic problems, and is the solution sought in the weak form.

concepts_list.name: test_function_v
concepts_list.description: A test function used in the weak formulation, belonging to the function space V, and is varied over the domain to enforce the variational equation.
concepts_list.justification: The variable v appears in multiple weak formulations as the test function, with the requirement that the bilinear form B(u,v) holds for all v ∈ V.

concepts_list.name: stress_tensor_sigma_u
concepts_list.description: The Cauchy stress tensor computed from the strain field via the constitutive relation σ(u) = Cε(u).
concepts_list.justification: The expression σ(u) is explicitly defined as the stress tensor, formed from the constitutive tensor C and the strain tensor ε(u), and is used in the elastostatics weak form.

concepts_list.name: strain_tensor_epsilon_v
concepts_list.description: The linearized strain tensor computed from the displacement field, defined as ε(v) = 1/2 (∇v + ∇v^T).
concepts_list.justification: The strain tensor ε(v) is explicitly defined as ε(v) = 1/2 (∇v + ∇v^T), and is used in the elastostatics formulation as the input to the stress tensor.

concepts_list.name: boundary_Gamma_t
concepts_list.description: The portion of the boundary where Neumann boundary conditions (e.g., traction) are prescribed.
concepts_list.justification: Γ_t is explicitly defined as the boundary where Neumann boundary conditions are applied, such as prescribed heat flux or traction.

concepts_list.name: body_force_b
concepts_list.description: The body force (e.g., gravitational or inertial force) acting within the domain, appearing in the weak form of elastostatics.
concepts_list.justification: The body force b is introduced in the elastostatics weak form as a source term, and is integrated over the domain with the test function v.

concepts_list.name: traction_t_bar
concepts_list.description: The prescribed traction (e.g., surface force) on the boundary Γ_t, appearing in the boundary integral of the weak form.
concepts_list.justification: The traction t is used in the boundary integral with the test function v, and is denoted with a macron (overline), indicating it is a prescribed boundary condition.

concepts_list.name: integration_domain_d_Omega
concepts_list.description: The differential element for integration over the domain Ω, used in volume integrals.
concepts_list.justification: The symbol dΩ appears in volume integrals over Ω, such as ∫_Ω ∇v ⋅ (∇u) dΩ, indicating it is the integration variable over the domain.

concepts_list.name: integration_boundary_d_Gamma
concepts_list.description: The differential element for integration over the boundary Γ, used in surface integrals.
concepts_list.justification: The symbol dΓ appears in boundary integrals over Γ_t, such as ∫_{Γ_t} v ⋅ t_bar dΓ, indicating it is the surface integration element.

concepts_list.name: function_space_V
concepts_list.description: The function space of test functions, which must be chosen to satisfy the boundary conditions and is used in the weak formulation.
concepts_list.justification: The space V is repeatedly referenced as the space of test functions, with the requirement that the weak form holds for all v ∈ V.

domain_Omega.description:
The main computational domain in the physical space, representing the region where the problem is defined.
JUSTIFICATION:
The domain is explicitly defined as Ω ⊂ R^d, and is referred to multiple times as the domain of interest, with subdomains and boundaries derived from it.

primal_variable_u.description:
The primary unknown field variable, such as temperature or displacement, depending on the problem context.
JUSTIFICATION:
The symbol u is used throughout the paper to denote the primal variable, whether in thermal or elastostatic problems, and is the solution sought in the weak form.

test_function_v.description:
A test function used in the weak formulation, belonging to the function space V, and is varied over the domain to enforce the variational equation.
JUSTIFICATION:
The variable v appears in multiple weak formulations as the test function, with the requirement that the bilinear form B(u,v) holds for all v ∈ V.

stress_tensor_sigma_u.description:
The Cauchy stress tensor computed from the strain field via the constitutive relation σ(u) = Cε(u).
JUSTIFICATION:
The expression σ(u) is explicitly defined as the stress tensor, formed from the constitutive tensor C and the strain tensor ε(u), and is used in the elastostatics weak form.

strain_tensor_epsilon_v.description:
The linearized strain tensor computed from the displacement field, defined as ε(v) = 1/2 (∇v + ∇v^T).
JUSTIFICATION:
The strain tensor ε(v) is explicitly defined as ε(v) = 1/2 (∇v + ∇v^T), and is used in the elastostatics formulation as the input to the stress tensor.

boundary_Gamma_t.description:
The portion of the boundary where Neumann boundary conditions (e.g., traction) are prescribed.
JUSTIFICATION:
Γ_t is explicitly defined as the boundary where Neumann boundary conditions are applied, such as prescribed heat flux or traction.

body_force_b.description:
The body force (e.g., gravitational or inertial force) acting within the domain, appearing in the weak form of elastostatics.
JUSTIFICATION:
The body force b is introduced in the elastostatics weak form as a source term, and is integrated over the domain with the test function v.

traction_t_bar.description:
The prescribed traction (e.g., surface force) on the boundary Γ_t, appearing in the boundary integral of the weak form.

The traction t is used in the boundary integral with the test function v, and is denoted with a macron (overline), indicating it is a prescribed boundary condition.

The symbol dΩ appears in volume integrals over Ω, such as ∫_Ω ∇v ⋅ (∇u) dΩ, indicating it is the integration variable over the domain.

integration_boundary_d_Gamma.description:
The differential element for integration over the boundary Γ, used in surface integrals.
JUSTIFICATION:
The symbol dΓ appears in boundary integrals over Γ_t, such as ∫_{Γ_t} v ⋅ t_bar dΓ, indicating it is the surface integration element.

function_space_V.description:
The function space of test functions, which must be chosen to satisfy the boundary conditions and is used in the weak formulation.
JUSTIFICATION:
The space V is repeatedly referenced as the space of test functions, with the requirement that the weak form holds for all v ∈ V.
